text: Polls, politicians, and the markets. Many were sure the UK would vote to stay in the European Union. Then came the results. Let June the 23rd go down in history as our Independence Day. The British people have voted to leave the European Union. Investors were shocked. The pound has been hammered, and the markets have also fallen sharply. Sterling hit a 30-year low, and British Prime Minister David Cameron resigned. We will invoke Article 50 no later than the end of March next year. Leaving new Prime Minister Theresa May in charge of Brexit. But would it be a hard Brexit, a soft Brexit, or something more colourful? It's about a red, white, and blue Brexit. Whatever form it takes, one thing is clear. In 2017, words will have to become action.
